name: Framer API
what_it_does: Framer is a visual web builder with a CMS and REST API — agents can manage site content, publish pages, and interact with Framer's headless CMS programmatically.
use_cases: Managing CMS content on a Framer-built website from an agent
Programmatically publishing or updating web pages via CMS API
Automating content workflows (draft, review, publish cycles)
Fetching published content for downstream processing
not_for: Complex web application backends (Framer is a site builder, not an app framework)
Developer teams needing git-based workflows (Framer is GUI-first)
Building agent interfaces or dashboards (use React/Next.js directly)
best_when: You're managing content on a Framer-hosted marketing site and need to automate content updates from an agent.
avoid_when: You need a full-featured CMS with complex content modeling — Framer CMS is optimized for marketing sites, not complex data structures.

auth.notes: Project-level API tokens from Framer dashboard. Limited scope — tied to specific site/project.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for up to 3 projects with Framer branding and no custom domain
pricing.paid_tiers: Mini ($5/month), Basic ($15/month), Pro ($30/month) — API access on paid plans
pricing.notes: CMS API requires paid hosting plan. API rate limits not prominently documented.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation is limited and sometimes out of date. Error messages are basic HTTP status codes without detailed context.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support on content operations.
known_agent_gotchas: API is relatively limited compared to specialized headless CMS platforms
Rate limits are not well-documented — agents should implement conservative backoff
CMS field types are limited — complex content modeling is not supported
No webhook support for triggering agents on content changes
API changes without much notice — treat as unstable API

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API tokens are project-scoped but there is no fine-grained permission control. Token rotation requires manual dashboard action.
